aliases: Chicken Garlic Sausage Pork Chorizo Beef Bratwurst Platter

english: Chicken Garlic Sausage Pork Chorizo Beef Bratwurst Platter

region_of_origin: Singapore (restaurant adaptation; sausage types of European origin)
food_subgroup: Mixed meat platter with sides (sausages, mashed potato, salad)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a mixed multi-sausage platter with sides. FOODON_03315116 (sausage food product) is the closest parent class covering the dominant components.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers sausages and similar products; no specific code exists for a mixed platter combining chicken garlic sausage, pork chorizo, beef bratwurst with mashed potato and salad.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for a mixed sausage platter dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One platter (approximately 450 g) comprising three types of sausage (chicken garlic, pork chorizo, beef bratwurst), a portion of mashed potato, and a side salad.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Sausage, pork, fresh, cooked (SR Legacy 10165); Sausage, chicken, cooked (SR Legacy 05338); Chorizo, pork and beef, cooked (SR Legacy 07064); Mashed potato, home-prepared with whole milk and butter (SR Legacy 11371); Salad greens, mixed (SR Legacy 11959) — weighted composite estimate
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for this mixed sausage platter. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the three sausage types (chicken garlic, pork chorizo, beef bratwurst), mashed potato, and mixed salad greens. Assumed platter composition by weight: chicken garlic sausage 25%, pork chorizo 25%, beef bratwurst 25%, mashed potato 20%, salad 5%. Per-100g values derived from this weighted blend. Values are estimates and should be verified against the specific restaurant preparation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS, Brand-Miller JC, Foster-Powell K, Buyken AE, Goletzke J. American Journal of Clinical Nutrition 2021; analogous estimate based on mixed meat and potato dish
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a mixed sausage platter. GI estimated at 40 based on the dominant glycemic contributor being mashed potato (GI ~87 per University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021) diluted by the large proportion of protein- and fat-rich sausage components which have negligible GI. The blended dish GI is estimated conservatively at 40 (low category) reflecting the mixed macronutrient composition. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 36.45) / 100 = 14.6, where carbohydrate per 450g serving = 8.1 × 4.5 = 36.45g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from processed sausages (approximately 620 mg sodium per 100g; ~2790 mg per platter)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this dish due to its very high sodium load from processed sausages including chorizo and bratwurst, which can significantly exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg in a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (GL ~14.6 per serving) with high saturated fat from processed meats
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this dish with caution; while the glycemic load is moderate due to the protein and fat buffering effect, the high saturated fat content from chorizo and bratwurst may worsen insulin resistance and cardiovascular risk, and portion control is strongly advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, high sodium, and processed meat content associated with increased cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This dish is high in saturated fat (~5.4g/100g) and sodium (~620mg/100g) from multiple processed sausage types, and regular consumption of processed red and mixed meats is associated with increased cardiovascular disease risk per WHO and AHA dietary guidelines; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Sausage Platter is a hearty mixed-meat dish served at #Foodcoholic restaurant in Singapore, featuring three distinct sausage varieties: chicken garlic sausage, pork chorizo, and beef bratwurst. These sausages are typically grilled or pan-fried and served alongside creamy mashed potato and a fresh side salad, making it a substantial Western-style meal. The chicken garlic sausage offers a mild, aromatic flavour; the pork chorizo contributes smoky, paprika-spiced richness; and the beef bratwurst provides a coarser, savoury texture characteristic of German-style sausages. The mashed potato adds a creamy, starchy counterpoint, while the salad provides freshness and textural contrast. Nutritionally, this dish is energy-dense (approximately 218 kcal per 100g) with a high protein content (~12.5g/100g) and significant fat (~15.2g/100g), of which saturated fat is notable (~5.4g/100g). Sodium content is high (~620mg/100g) due to the processed nature of all three sausage types. The glycemic load per serving is moderate (~14.6) as the protein and fat components buffer the glycemic impact of the mashed potato. Clinically, this dish raises concern for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease due to its processed meat sodium and saturated fat load. It is not suitable for halal or kosher diets due to the pork chorizo component. Priced at SGD 28.00, it represents a premium casual-dining Western fusion offering in the Singapore food service market.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific mixed sausage platter. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for the individual components. The GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level USDA data is high quality and the weighting assumptions are reasonable for a standard platter of this type.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy component entries used for this composite estimate. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the cited USDA SR Legacy source entries. The cuisine tag 'Indian' in the input record appears to be a field survey platform categorisation error; this dish is Western/fusion in character and has been reclassified accordingly. The cuisine_tags field from the input ('Indian', 'Chicken Dish') are preserved as context but do not reflect the actual culinary classification. Multilingual names are provided per Singaporean dish requirements as this dish is served in Singapore. Halal status is false due to the presence of pork chorizo. Serving size of 450g is an estimate based on typical restaurant platter portions for three sausages with sides; actual restaurant portion may vary.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Drafted full record. Identified that cuisine tag 'Indian' from input was a platform error; reclassified as Western/Fusion. Applied Singaporean dish multilingual rules (English, Chinese simplified+traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi). Computed weighted composite nutrients from USDA SR Legacy. Estimated GI at 40 using analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database. Documented all -1 sentinel values in notes. Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 passed on first scoring pass.

Iteration 2: Final review confirmed aliases consistency — all aliases_by_language strings (香肠拼盘, 香腸拼盤, Pinggan Sosej, Sosej Pelbagai, சாஸேஜ் தட்டு, सॉसेज प्लेटर, Xiāngcháng pīnpán, and all English variants) present verbatim in flat aliases array. Score confirmed 8/8. Decision: PASS.
